ordinary_no_progress_guard.fingerprint: ordinary|bener, sebelumnya filler hitam di atas background hitam jadi cuma kelihatan seperti border. sudah saya ubah. sekarang: - area kosong di luar data diisi hex cells putih/abu, jadi benar-benar “fill the empty”. - radius filler diperluas dari `code.radius + 3` ke `code.radius + 4`. - frame ditarik lebih rapat ke hasil filler, jadi sisa hitam besar berkurang. - filler punya variasi abu tipis supaya tidak terlihat seperti outline doang. validasi lewat: - `npm test` - `npm run build` - `npm run lint`

ordinary|improved the density comparison panel with a clear winner and deeper analysis. changed: - [densitycomparison.tsx](/users/faistech/wwjs/hexqr/src/components/densitycomparison.tsx:28): added density ratio, utilization, spare capacity, and winner logic. - [densitycomparison.tsx](/users/faistech/wwjs/hexqr/src/components/densitycomparison.tsx:182): added winner summary and analysis metric cards. - [styles.css](/users/faistech/wwjs/hexqr/src/styles.css:601): added responsive grid rules for the new analysis layout. validation: `npm run build` passes. dev server is running at `http://127.0.0.1:5173/`.

ordinary|added [.gitignore](/users/faistech/wwjs/hexqr/.gitignore:1) for the current vite/typescript project. it now ignores `node_modules/`, `dist/`, build/test artifacts, logs, env files, editor folders, macos `.ds_store`, and typescript build info. verified with `git status --short`: `node_modules/`, `dist/`, and `.ds_store` no longer appear as untracked.

ordinary|fixed that marker-line bug. in [src/components/hexcodecanvas.tsx](/users/faistech/wwjs/hexqr/src/components/hexcodecanvas.tsx), the outer border strokes now render behind the hex cells instead of on top of them. that keeps the perimeter border visible but prevents those extra lines from cutting across finder markers on non-classic themes. verified: - `npm run lint` passes - `npm run build` passes

ordinary|not for the benchmark we’re using. a regular hexagon is efficient as a **shape**, but scanners and print layouts reserve a **rectangular bounding box**. a regular hex outer shape only fills about `75%` of its bounding rectangle. the corner space is empty, but the camera/print footprint still pays for it. so: - **hex outer silhouette**: better identity, more “hexlattice”, nicer orientation symmetry. - **box-like offset hex grid**: more data cells per printed rectangular area, better print density. - **individual cells are still hexagons** in v3; only the outer envelope became rectangular. for replacing qr code, the rectangle was chosen because qr is also measured inside a square/rectangular module footprint. if we switch v3 back to a true hex silhouette, it will look more correct conceptually, but print density will likely drop unless we compensate with more states, less structure, or a larger symbol. a good compromise is probably a **chamfered hex-rectangle**: mostly rectangular for density, but clipped corners/fiducial corners so the full shape reads as hexagonal instead of a plain box.

ordinary|fixed the main performance issue: wasm was failing to decode, then js fallback was doing the real work. rust now matches the js mask/ec sweep strategy when metadata is unreliable. changes: - fixed rust metadata order mismatch. - added safe metadata bounds handling. - added mask/ec sweep inside wasm. - wasm now decodes the sample directly in local probe: `https://example.com/hexcode`, warm runs roughly `11-30ms`. - debug log now reports decoder source: `via wasm`, `via js`, or `via object`. verified: - `npm run build` passes. - focused tests pass: `encoderdecoder`, `v3format`, `densitycomparison`. you should now see `/decode` log successful sample decodes as `via wasm` instead of silently falling through to js.
